description_long: This is a weathered stone sculpture of a standing male figure, depicted in a traditional, ancient Egyptian style. The figure is missing its head and left forearm, but the right arm is intact and held close to the body. The figure wears a short kilt and stands with the left leg forward, a common pose in Egyptian statuary. The back of the sculpture features a vertical support pillar with carved hieroglyphic inscriptions. The entire piece is mounted on a rectangular stone base, which is itself placed on a larger, flat platform. The surface of the sculpture is rough and pitted, showing signs of age and exposure.
description: A weathered stone statue of a standing male figure in ancient Egyptian style, missing its head and left forearm, with hieroglyphs on the back support.
description_view_0: Side view showing the figure's left leg stepping forward and the vertical support pillar with hieroglyphs.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the missing head and left forearm, with the right arm close to the body and the left leg forward.
description_view_2: Side view from the opposite side, showing the right profile and the support pillar behind the figure.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the full length of the support pillar with visible hieroglyphic inscriptions.
attribute_subject_matter: human figure in ancient Egyptian style
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is mounted on a display platform, suggesting an indoor exhibition or museum setting.
